decisions: Retained memory/daily/2026-04-21.md (2 days old, bootstrap log only, within 7-day window) — no promotable user-session content found
Retained memory/daily/2026-04-22.md (yesterday, mandatory retention) — contains only consolidation records, no user content to promote
Skipped all tier updates (semantic, episodic, procedural, core) — reviewed all 3 daily logs and found zero user-interaction entries; all entries are maintenance/consolidation records
observations: Brain has been active for 3 days (04-21 through 04-23) with zero user sessions — all activity is automated heartbeat consolidation
Memory tiers are structurally sound but completely empty of substantive content
Daily log coverage is 100% for days with sessions, but all entries are consolidation metadata only
recommendations: If no user sessions occur within the next 3 days, consider reducing consolidation frequency to avoid accumulating identical no-op reports
First real user interaction should be captured thoroughly to seed all memory tiers
processImprovements: [self-critique] Three consolidation runs in one day with zero user activity produces diminishing returns — each run generates nearly identical reports. The maintenance guard's 24h threshold should prevent this, but multiple heartbeats within the same day still trigger separate runs. The current process lacks a 'nothing new since last run today' short-circuit.

[proposal] Add a same-day deduplication check: if a consolidation report for today already exists and no non-maintenance commits have occurred since, skip the run entirely or produce a minimal 'no-change' report instead of a full one.
[blocked] The maintenance-guard.sh script controls run frequency but doesn't account for multiple heartbeats per day. This may need a base-brain change to add an intra-day cooldown.
